claim: Toxic plant folios (15 specified Latin names, 14 with >=20 tokens) show elevated _.oii vowel-pattern fire rate: toxic 1.08% vs non-toxic 0.34% (3.15x ratio). One-tailed Welch's t-test p = 0.060, Cohen's d = 0.73, Hedges' g = 0.72. Bootstrap 95% CI on mean difference: [-0.00006, +0.01649] — misses excluding zero by 6 parts per million. The pre-registered alpha=0.05 threshold is NOT met, but the effect size is medium-to-large and the direction matches prediction. Signal is bimodal: 5 of 14 toxic folios fire heavily (Paris quadrifolia 4.2%, Rhododendron 4.8%, Delphinium 1.9%, Euphorbia 1.7%, Cuscuta 1.6%, Nymphaea 0.9%), 9 fire at zero.
evidence_for: Directional t = +1.65 in the predicted direction
Ratio 3.15x, same order as plant-vs-non-plant _.oii enrichment (4.84x)
5 toxic folios carry strong signal (1.6-4.8% _.oii rate)
evidence_against: p = 0.060 fails the pre-registered alpha = 0.05 threshold
95% CI barely includes zero — by only 6 ppm but it includes it
9 of 14 toxic folios fire _.oii at zero rate — signal is not uniformly toxic-linked
Sample is underpowered (n=14 toxic, need ~20 for 80% power at d=0.73)
Bimodal distribution suggests 'toxic' may not be the right sub-class; the 5 firing plants may share something else (growth habit, preparation, etc.)
test: Acquire 6+ more toxic plant folio identifications to reach n>=20 toxic, then re-run one-tailed t-test. Alternatively, study what the 5 firing toxic plants share that the 9 non-firing don't.
test_threshold: p < 0.05 with n>=20 toxic: confirmed illustration-content signal. p >= 0.10 at larger n: effect shrinks, hypothesis refuted.

tests_run.details: p=0.060 one-tailed, d=0.73, 3.15x ratio. Direction correct, effect meaningful, but pre-registered threshold missed by 0.010. Bimodal: 5 toxic folios carry signal, 9 zero.

tests_remaining: Acquire more toxic plant IDs (6+ needed for 80% power)
Sub-cluster analysis: what do the 5 firing toxic plants share?
Test other medicinal sub-classes (sedative, cardioactive, etc.)
reasoning: Pre-registered test fell 0.01 short of significance. Brain-V publishes the marginal result rather than burying it. The discipline says: direction and effect size are real, but the pre-specified threshold was not met, so this is NOT yet evidence of illustration-content encoding. Hypothesis stays active at modest confidence pending more data.
